worldFightersCodes.title: World Fighters Codes
worldFightersCodes.subtitle: Redeem World Fighters codes for potions, gems, Dragon Balls Keys, Saiyan Keys, and special rewards. Start with the newest Release Pt3 codes before claiming older milestone codes.
worldFightersCodes.intro: World Fighters codes are released around updates, visit milestones, like milestones, CCU milestones, and shutdown compensation. The most useful early rewards are Power Potion, Crystal Potion, Damage Potion, Luck Potion, gems, and keys that help you unlock avatars, pets, and faster progression.

redeemGuide.label: World Fighters Code Redemption Steps
steps: Press the Shop button on the left side of the screen.
Scroll to the code box near the bottom of the Shop menu.
Paste one code exactly as shown and press Redeem.

summary: Redeem newest Release Pt3 and milestone codes first.
Potion rewards are most useful while actively farming enemies.
Keys and gems help accelerate early unlocks, avatars, pets, and gacha attempts.

worldFightersBeginnerGuide.title: World Fighters Beginner Guide
worldFightersBeginnerGuide.subtitle: Learn the early loop: defeat enemies, collect Cristals, unlock avatars and pets, use codes, and push into new worlds. This route is built for players starting from their first session.
worldFightersBeginnerGuide.intro: World Fighters is an anime-inspired Roblox simulator where players defeat enemies, collect Cristals, unlock avatars and pets, and complete challenges with friends. New players should focus on redeeming codes, improving damage and power, opening early rewards, and clearing map quests that unlock the next zone.

steps.title: Start from the official Roblox experience
steps.goal: Join World Fighters from the official StarX Inc game page.
steps.details: The game supports PC, mobile, tablet, and console, so new players can start on any major Roblox device. Use the official page first to avoid fake copies and outdated clone experiences.

steps.title: Redeem codes before grinding
steps.goal: Claim potions, gems, and keys before fighting stronger enemies.
steps.details: Codes give early boosts such as Power Potion, Crystal Potion, Damage Potion, Luck Potion, Gems, Dragon Balls Keys, and Saiyan Keys. Redeem the newest Release Pt3 and milestone codes first because update and compensation codes are the highest-priority freebies.

steps.title: Defeat enemies to collect Cristals
steps.goal: Build your first progression loop around enemy farming.
steps.details: World Fighters describes its core loop as defeating enemies to collect Cristals. Use early boosts when you are actively farming so your power, damage, luck, and crystal gain bonuses turn into faster progress.

steps.title: Unlock avatars and pets
steps.goal: Use your earned resources to expand your roster.
steps.details: Avatars and pets are part of the main unlock path in World Fighters. New players should treat early gems, Cristals, stars, and gacha rolls as progression tools instead of spending everything randomly.

steps.title: Push map quests to unlock new zones
steps.goal: Complete the current map objective and move to the next world.
steps.details: Progressing to a new map revolves around completing the main quest in the current area and earning the required key. Zone badges such as Zone 1, Zone 2, Zone 3, Zone 4, Zone 5, and Zone 6 make zone progress easy to track.

steps.title: Use friends and shared rewards
steps.goal: Play with friends to finish challenges more efficiently.
steps.details: World Fighters supports friend play and shared rewards across game modes. Group play is useful when enemies take longer to defeat, when challenges require consistency, or when you want to speed up repeated farming sessions.

steps.title: Track first-time milestones
steps.goal: Use badges as a simple checklist for early progression.
steps.details: Early progression milestones include Welcome to World Fighters, First Star Open, First Gacha Roll, First Secret Unit, First Secret Gacha, First Awakening, and zone-clear badges. These milestones show whether your account is still in the opening phase or ready for deeper farming.

checklist: Start from the official Roblox experience.
Redeem codes before long farming sessions.
Use boosts while defeating enemies for Cristals.
Spend early resources on avatars, pets, stars, and gacha progress.
Complete map quests to move through new zones.

worldFightersOfficialLinks.title: World Fighters Discord and Official Links
worldFightersOfficialLinks.subtitle: Use these links to play World Fighters, join the StarX Inc community, find the Discord, and check game stats. Official links are separated from third-party code and analytics pages.
worldFightersOfficialLinks.intro: World Fighters is published on Roblox by StarX Inc. The official Roblox experience and StarX Inc community are the primary starting points, while the Discord invite is used for updates, codes, and player discussion.

links.name: World Fighters Roblox Experience
links.useCase: Play the game, view the Store tab, check Servers, and launch World Fighters from the official Roblox page.

links.name: StarX Inc Roblox Community
links.useCase: Follow the developer group behind World Fighters and related Roblox experiences.

links.name: World Fighters Simulator Discord
links.useCase: Join the World Fighters Discord for update notices, new code drops, community chat, and game announcements.

links.name: Rolimon's World Fighters Page
links.useCase: Check live players, visits, rating, favorites, average playtime, peak CCU, gamepasses, badges, and update timing.

links.name: Pocket Tactics World Fighters Codes
links.useCase: Check a frequently updated code list with rewards and redemption instructions.

links.name: Try Hard Guides World Fighters Codes
links.useCase: Check working codes, expired codes, redemption steps, and code troubleshooting tips.

links.name: Pro Game Guides World Fighters Codes
links.useCase: Check active codes, code rewards, redemption steps, and official community link references.

worldFightersTierList.title: World Fighters Tier List
worldFightersTierList.subtitle: Rank World Fighters gamepasses by early progression value, farming speed, gacha convenience, and long-term utility. This tier list focuses on real gamepass names and prices visible in public Roblox tracking data.
worldFightersTierList.intro: World Fighters has a large gamepass set covering direct progression boosts, luck boosts, opening speed, spin speed, storage, VIP, and utility. For most players, direct farming power and crystal gain come first, then luck and opening speed, then storage and situational convenience.

entries.name: 2x Damage
entries.whyItMatters: Directly improves enemy clear speed, making it one of the strongest early and mid-game progression purchases.

entries.name: 2x Power
entries.whyItMatters: Improves core strength growth and helps players push through stronger enemies faster.

entries.name: 2x Crystals
entries.whyItMatters: Boosts the main farming resource used in the World Fighters progression loop.

entries.name: Extra Drops
entries.whyItMatters: Adds value to repeated enemy farming and long grind sessions.

entries.name: Luck
entries.whyItMatters: Low-cost luck option for players who want better roll value without buying the larger luck passes first.

entries.name: Super Luck
entries.whyItMatters: A stronger luck upgrade for players opening stars, rolling gacha, and chasing better unlocks.

entries.name: Mega Luck
entries.whyItMatters: Best suited for players who roll frequently and want a premium luck-focused upgrade.

entries.name: Extra Equip
entries.whyItMatters: Extra equip value increases as the player unlocks stronger avatars, pets, and units.

entries.name: Fast Open
entries.whyItMatters: Useful for players who repeatedly open stars or rewards and want less downtime.

entries.name: Multi Open
entries.whyItMatters: Improves repeated opening sessions and pairs well with luck-focused upgrades.

entries.name: Fast Spin
entries.whyItMatters: Saves time during repeated spin-based actions.

entries.name: Multi Spin
entries.whyItMatters: Useful for players who spend a lot of time on spin systems and want a faster loop.

entries.name: Small Storage
entries.whyItMatters: Cheap quality-of-life option for new players who want extra room early.

entries.name: Big Storage
entries.whyItMatters: Better for players who collect more avatars, pets, and unlocks over time.

entries.name: VIP
entries.whyItMatters: A broader premium option for players who want VIP benefits instead of a single focused boost.

entries.name: Remote Star
entries.whyItMatters: More useful after players understand the star and opening loop and want remote convenience.

entries.name: Dual Sword
entries.whyItMatters: Best treated as a later purchase after core farming, damage, power, and resource boosts.

buyingNotes: Prioritize direct farming boosts before situational convenience.
Luck passes gain value when you are opening stars or chasing rare unlocks.
Storage and remote utility are comfort picks after core progression boosts.

worldFightersSecretUnitsGuide.title: World Fighters Secret Units Guide
worldFightersSecretUnitsGuide.subtitle: Learn why Secret units matter, how Secret Luffy fits into late-game progression, and which resources help you chase rare pulls more efficiently.
worldFightersSecretUnitsGuide.intro: Secret units are one of the biggest long-term goals in World Fighters because they sit at the top of the collection grind and connect directly with gacha rolls, luck boosts, keys, stars, and awakening progression. Build a resource routine first, then use gems, luck rewards, and faster opening tools when you are ready to chase rare pulls.

items.title: Secret Luffy Target
items.description: Secret Luffy is a major high-end chase unit in World Fighters. Players searching for Secret Luffy should focus on resource farming, gacha access, luck boosts, and late-game progression before spending gems heavily.

items.title: First Secret Unit Badge
items.description: The First Secret Unit badge is an important collection milestone that sits far beyond early actions such as opening stars or rolling gacha. Treat it as a long-term target supported by stronger farming routes.

items.title: First Secret Gacha Badge
items.description: First Secret Gacha belongs in the late-game chase layer. Approach Secret Gacha after your damage, resources, roll access, and luck support are stable enough for repeated pull sessions.

items.title: Luck Boost Stack
items.description: Codes commonly reward Luck Potions, while the game store includes Luck, Super Luck, and Mega Luck boosts. These are most valuable when used around banner rolls, gacha sessions, star openings, or Secret Unit attempts.

items.title: Keys and Resource Support
items.tag: Dragon Balls Key and Saiyan Key
items.description: Many active codes reward Dragon Balls Keys, Saiyan Keys, gems, and potions. These rewards reduce early farming pressure and help players reach stronger zones and better pull opportunities faster.

items.title: Secret Boss and Progression Route
items.description: Secret unit hunting works best after players have a stable damage base, more unlocked zones, and a repeatable route for bosses or high-value targets. Treat Secret hunting as a progression layer, not the first place to spend every reward.

routine: Redeem codes and bank gems, luck potions, and keys before long pull sessions.
Push zones and awakening progress until farming routes are fast enough to sustain repeated rolls.
Spend rare-pull boosts during planned gacha or star sessions instead of casual early farming.

worldFightersGachaAndStarGuide.title: World Fighters Gacha and Star Guide
worldFightersGachaAndStarGuide.subtitle: Understand how Star Open, Gacha Roll, banners, gems, and luck rewards connect before spending your resources.
worldFightersGachaAndStarGuide.intro: World Fighters uses a familiar anime-simulator loop: farm resources, open stars or roll gacha, improve your collection, then push stronger zones. Gems are used for in-game banners, while stars, gacha rolls, luck potions, and opening-speed upgrades shape how efficiently you chase better avatars, pets, and rare units.

items.title: Claim Code Rewards First
items.action: Redeem active World Fighters codes before rolling because many codes give gems, potions, and keys.
items.whyItMatters: Codes can supply gems for banners, Luck Potions for rare pulls, and keys such as Dragon Balls Keys or Saiyan Keys, giving new players a faster start.

items.title: Open Your First Star Early
items.action: Use the Star system once you have enough resources and want to begin building your avatar or pet collection.
items.whyItMatters: The first Star Open milestone is a natural early progression step before deeper Secret milestones and long rare-pull sessions.

items.title: Use Gacha Rolls for Banner Progress
items.action: Spend gems on banner or gacha rolls when the available rewards match your World Fighters progression goal.
items.whyItMatters: Gems are tied to in-game banners that can unlock customization and roster options, so planned spending keeps long-term progress cleaner.

items.title: Save Luck for Rare-Pull Sessions
items.action: Use Luck Potions and luck-focused boosts when you are doing a serious roll session, not during casual early farming.
items.whyItMatters: Luck rewards are most valuable when paired with banners, stars, gacha rolls, or Secret Unit attempts.

items.title: Improve Opening Efficiency
items.action: Consider Fast Open, Multi Open, and Remote Star after you know you will farm rolls often.
items.whyItMatters: Opening-related upgrades matter most during long gacha or star-opening sessions where saved time compounds across many rolls.

items.title: Chase Secret Gacha Later
items.action: Treat First Secret Gacha as a late-game milestone after your farming route, damage, and resource flow are stable.
items.whyItMatters: Secret Gacha is a long-term World Fighters target that becomes more efficient when supported by prepared resources and luck boosts.

worldFightersAwakeningGuide.title: World Fighters Awakening Guide
worldFightersAwakeningGuide.subtitle: Awakening is a permanent growth layer that turns raw farming into stronger long-term damage and power progression.
worldFightersAwakeningGuide.intro: World Fighters awakenings provide permanent damage and power multiplier spikes, and later awakenings require more power to unlock. Use awakening as a planned progression cycle: farm power, clear zones, unlock the next awakening, then repeat with stronger units and better resource flow.

items.title: Build Base Power
items.description: Start by training, clicking, defeating enemies, and completing early quests so your fighter has enough strength to push into stronger content.
items.focus: Power farming and early enemy clears

items.title: Clear Map Quests
items.description: Progressing to new worlds usually requires completing the main quest for your current map and earning the key needed to unlock the next area teleport.

items.title: Unlock First Awakening
items.description: The first awakening is a major early-to-mid progression marker that gives the account a permanent multiplier spike and makes the next farming cycle faster.

items.title: Repeat Awakening Cycles
items.description: Each successive awakening requires more power but gives a stronger permanent boost. This makes awakening valuable before deeper zone farming, boss clears, and rare-unit chasing.

items.title: Pair Awakening With Secret Hunts
items.description: After your awakening path is stable, Secret units and Secret Gacha become more realistic long-term goals because you can farm harder content and resources faster.

worldFightersCrystalsAndGemsGuide.title: World Fighters Crystals and Gems Guide
worldFightersCrystalsAndGemsGuide.subtitle: Track what Crystals, Gems, Potions, and Keys are used for so you do not waste early-game rewards.
worldFightersCrystalsAndGemsGuide.intro: World Fighters progression is built around defeating enemies, collecting Crystals, unlocking avatars and pets, and using code rewards to speed up the early grind. Gems are especially important because they connect to in-game banners and rolls, while potions and keys help players farm faster or unlock progress routes sooner.

items.mainUses: Core progression, avatar and pet unlock flow, and early farming growth
items.bestUse: Spend on progression that improves your farming speed before chasing rare late-game goals.

items.mainSources: Codes, milestone rewards, and progression rewards
items.mainUses: In-game banners, gacha rolls, avatars, and pets
items.bestUse: Save gems for banners or roll sessions that directly improve your team instead of spending them immediately.

items.mainSources: Many active World Fighters codes include one of each potion
items.mainUses: Improving rare-pull efficiency during gacha, star, or banner sessions
items.bestUse: Use before planned rare-pull sessions, especially when chasing Secret units or better banner rewards.
Activate before enemy-grind sessions where you can farm continuously.

items.mainUses: Speeding up power growth and awakening preparation
items.bestUse: Use when you are actively training or pushing toward an awakening requirement.

items.mainUses: Helping defeat stronger enemies, bosses, or challenge targets faster
items.bestUse: Save for hard fights, new zones, or boss routes instead of using during easy farming.

items.mainSources: Many active World Fighters codes, including visit and like milestone codes
items.mainUses: Key-based progression and Dragon Ball-themed reward access
items.bestUse: Keep and use when the related world, quest, or reward path becomes available.

items.mainSources: Shutdown compensation codes such as SRRY4SHUTDOWN, SRRY4SHUTDOWN2, and SRRY4SHUTDOWN3
items.mainUses: Saiyan-themed key progression or reward access
items.bestUse: Redeem shutdown codes early so the key is ready when you reach the matching content.

worldFightersPotionsGuide.title: World Fighters Potions Guide
worldFightersPotionsGuide.subtitle: Use Power, Crystal, Damage, and Luck Potions at the right moment to speed up farming, zone pushing, and banner pulls.
worldFightersPotionsGuide.intro: Potions are one of the most common World Fighters code rewards. Most current reward bundles give one of each potion, making them best used in planned sessions instead of opening them randomly while idle.

items.focus: Power growth and general progression speed
items.bestTiming: Use before long enemy-farming sessions, early zone pushes, or when building enough strength to clear the next area.

items.focus: Crystal farming for unlocks and roster progression
items.bestTiming: Use when repeatedly defeating enemies for Crystals, especially before saving for avatars, pets, or banner-related upgrades.
Use before bosses, harder challenges, or a new zone where enemies take too long to defeat.

items.focus: Luck-based rewards, rare pulls, and gacha preparation
items.bestTiming: Use right before Star Open, Gacha Roll, or rare-unit hunting sessions instead of during normal movement or menu time.

items.focus: One Power, Crystal, Damage, and Luck Potion together
items.bestTiming: Save full bundles for focused sessions: activate Damage and Power before pushing, Crystal before farming, and Luck before pulls.

worldFightersKeysAndTokensGuide.title: World Fighters Keys and Tokens Guide
worldFightersKeysAndTokensGuide.subtitle: Track Dragon Balls Keys, Dragon Ball Tokens, Saiyan Keys, Gems, and mount rewards from current World Fighters codes.
worldFightersKeysAndTokensGuide.intro: Keys and tokens are update-sensitive resources in World Fighters. They often appear inside milestone, shutdown, and release-patch code bundles, so players should claim them early and save them for the matching banner, event, or unlock screen.

items.name: Dragon Balls Key
items.howToUse: Prioritize saving Dragon Balls Keys for Dragon-themed unlock routes, special pulls, or update-specific progression instead of spending them immediately.

items.name: Dragon Ball Token
items.howToUse: Treat Dragon Ball Tokens as limited progression currency tied to Dragon-themed rewards. Stack them from code bundles before making larger unlock decisions.

items.name: Saiyan Key
items.howToUse: Saiyan Keys are mainly tied to shutdown-compensation style rewards. Save them until the related Saiyan-themed feature, banner, or unlock path is available in your session.

items.name: Gems
items.howToUse: Use Gems for in-game banner rolls that help unlock avatars and pets. Combine Gem spending with Luck Potion timing when chasing rarer pulls.

items.name: Gadi Jetpack Mount
items.howToUse: Claim the mount code as soon as possible because unique mount rewards are less common than standard potion-and-gem bundles.

worldFightersAvatarsAndPetsGuide.title: World Fighters Avatars and Pets Guide
worldFightersAvatarsAndPetsGuide.subtitle: Build your roster by farming Crystals, spending Gems on banners, and chasing exclusive pets that support long-term progression.
worldFightersAvatarsAndPetsGuide.intro: World Fighters is built around defeating enemies, collecting Crystals, and unlocking new avatars and exclusive pets. For new players, the safest approach is to improve your active roster first, then spend saved Gems during focused banner or gacha sessions.

items.name: Avatars
items.howToUnlock: Farm enemies for Crystals and use Gems on banners to unlock more character options.
items.beginnerTip: Focus on a stronger active avatar before spreading resources across too many characters.

items.name: Exclusive Pets
items.howToUnlock: Use banner-related resources and event rewards to chase pets alongside avatar unlocks.
items.beginnerTip: Do not ignore pets while pushing zones; they are part of the long-term roster chase.

items.name: Crystals
items.howToUnlock: Defeat enemies across anime-inspired worlds to collect Crystals.
items.beginnerTip: Use Crystal Potion before repeated enemy farming sessions to make unlock progress more efficient.

items.name: Gems
items.howToUnlock: Redeem active codes, complete progression goals, and claim milestone rewards.
items.beginnerTip: Save Gems for meaningful banner sessions instead of spending every small reward immediately.

items.name: First Star Open
items.howToUnlock: Open your first Star after reaching the required in-game system.
items.beginnerTip: Use this as the first step toward understanding avatar and pet pulls.

items.name: First Gacha Roll
items.howToUnlock: Roll once in the gacha system after collecting enough resources.
items.beginnerTip: Pair gacha attempts with Luck Potion when chasing rare units or pets.

items.name: Shared Challenges
items.howToUnlock: Play with friends and complete challenges together for shared rewards across game modes.
items.beginnerTip: Group play helps new players progress while learning harder zones.

worldFightersZonesProgressionGuide.title: World Fighters Zones Progression Guide
worldFightersZonesProgressionGuide.subtitle: Follow the Zone 1 to Zone 6 route and use badge data to understand where most players slow down.
worldFightersZonesProgressionGuide.intro: World Fighters zone progression gets much steeper after the starting area. Badge data shows a sharp drop from Zone 1 and Zone 2 into the later zones, so players should treat each new zone as a farming checkpoint before pushing forward.

items.progressionFocus: Learn enemy farming, collect Crystals, redeem early codes, and unlock your first roster upgrades.
items.checkpoint: Reach basic farming stability before spending heavily on pulls.

items.progressionFocus: Enemy clears start to slow down, so upgrade avatars and pets before rushing the next zone.
items.checkpoint: Farm until standard enemies no longer take too long to defeat.

items.progressionFocus: Start treating banner pulls, pet upgrades, and potion timing as part of normal progression.
items.checkpoint: Use Luck Potion before pull sessions and Crystal Potion before repeated farming.

items.progressionFocus: Push only after improving damage output and collecting enough resources for stronger roster options.
items.checkpoint: If enemies feel slow, farm the previous zone with boosts instead of forcing progress.

items.progressionFocus: Prepare with full potion bundles, stronger avatars, and better pet support before attempting long sessions.
items.checkpoint: Use this zone as a serious farming checkpoint before late-game content.

items.progressionFocus: Community interest centers on late Dragon-themed progression, rare units, and Secret Unit hunting at this stage.
items.checkpoint: Save Luck Potion for rare pulls and use Damage Potion when pushing the strongest enemies.

worldFightersRaidsGuide.title: World Fighters Raids Guide
worldFightersRaidsGuide.subtitle: Learn how Tempest Invasion works, when to spend Slime Keys, and how F2P players can use raids to strengthen their teams.
worldFightersRaidsGuide.intro: Raids in World Fighters currently center on Tempest Invasion, a wave-based mode added with Update 1. The mode connects Slime Keys, wave rewards, upgrades, achievements, and stronger unit farming into one repeatable late-game activity.
readinessChecklist: Unlock Tempest Federation before spending Slime Keys.
Farm enough Slime Keys for several Tempest Invasion attempts.
Upgrade Attack Distance before pushing deeper waves.
Use raid rewards to improve damage, luck, and future gacha sessions.
Treat each World Fighters raid run as a wave milestone push.

items.title: Reach Tempest Federation First
items.description: Tempest Invasion is tied to the Tempest Federation update area, so World Fighters players should progress through earlier zones and build a team strong enough to handle the new enemies.
items.player_goal: Unlock the new zone, start farming its enemies, and prepare your main lineup before entering raid waves.
items.best_for: Players who already have a stable World Fighters team and want to move into Update 1 progression.

items.title: Farm Slime Keys
items.description: Slime Keys are required to enter Tempest Invasion. Current World Fighters farming routes include enemy drops and secret boss drops in the Tempest Federation area.
items.player_goal: Build a small key stockpile before pushing multiple raid attempts.

items.title: Upgrade Range Before Deep Waves
items.description: Tempest Invasion sends enemies from multiple directions while the player holds the center. Attack Distance is especially valuable because it helps World Fighters units start hitting enemies earlier before they crowd the middle.
items.player_goal: Upgrade raid utility before relying only on raw damage.
items.best_for: F2P players trying to survive more waves through efficient World Fighters upgrades.

items.title: Push Wave Milestones
items.description: Tempest Invasion scales rewards by progress and includes achievement milestones from early waves up to wave 100. Reaching wave 10 to 15 is already a useful early benchmark for newer World Fighters raid players.
items.player_goal: Use each run to push one wave bracket higher, then reinvest rewards into upgrades and stronger units.

items.title: Turn Raid Rewards Into Team Power
items.description: Raid progress supports long-term World Fighters account growth through new currency, achievements, upgrades, and unit progression. F2P players should use raids as a repeatable path to improve damage, luck, and future gacha efficiency.
items.player_goal: Cycle between Tempest farming, raid attempts, upgrades, and banner pulls.
items.best_for: Players who want a non-Robux route to stronger units and better late-game scaling.

worldFightersSideQuestsAndChallenges.title: World Fighters Side Quests and Challenges
worldFightersSideQuestsAndChallenges.subtitle: Use this World Fighters checklist to separate main progression, side objectives, co-op challenges, and update activities.
worldFightersSideQuestsAndChallenges.intro: World Fighters is built around defeating enemies, collecting Cristals, unlocking avatars and pets, and completing challenges with friends. Side quests and challenges are best used as a second layer of progression while you farm zones, push bosses, and prepare for raids.

items.question: What is the World Fighters main progression loop?
items.answer: Defeat enemies to collect Cristals, then spend resources to unlock stronger avatars, pets, and units. This World Fighters loop should stay your first priority because every challenge becomes easier when your main team is stronger.

items.question: When should I do World Fighters side quests?
items.answer: Do side quests while farming a zone instead of treating them as separate chores. If a World Fighters quest objective overlaps with enemies, bosses, stars, or resource farming you already need, finish it before moving to the next area.

items.question: How do World Fighters co-op challenges help?
items.answer: World Fighters supports playing with friends and completing challenges together, with shared rewards across game modes. Co-op is useful when enemies take too long solo, when a boss route is slow, or when multiple players want to farm the same update content.

items.question: How are World Fighters raids different from normal challenges?
items.answer: Normal challenges support zone farming and account progression, while Tempest Invasion is a raid-style wave mode that uses Slime Keys and rewards deeper wave progress. Treat World Fighters raids as a dedicated activity after your team and range upgrades are ready.

items.question: Which World Fighters challenge should F2P players prioritize?
items.answer: F2P players should prioritize anything that improves long-term World Fighters farming speed: Cristals, gems, keys, upgrade currency, luck bonuses, and stronger units. Cosmetic rewards are better saved for later unless they come from the same route you are already farming.

items.question: Where does the World Fighters hidden boss fit into challenges?
items.answer: The hidden boss in Tempest Federation works like a high-value World Fighters challenge route. The route is near the Tempest Raid area, around the back-left side and hidden tunnel path, and rewards can include Slime Keys, Demon Tokens, wings, cosmetics, and Cristals.

worldFightersGamepassesGuide.title: World Fighters Gamepasses Guide
worldFightersGamepassesGuide.subtitle: Compare the main World Fighters gamepasses by farming value, gacha value, and convenience value before spending Robux.
worldFightersGamepassesGuide.intro: World Fighters gamepasses mostly improve farming speed, damage output, luck, storage, and summon convenience. For most players, the strongest value comes from direct progression boosts first, then luck and opening-speed passes after you are actively farming banners.

entries.best_for: World Fighters players who farm enemies often and want faster avatar, pet, and upgrade progression.
World Fighters players pushing zones, bosses, and raid waves where faster kills directly improve progression.
World Fighters players with multiple strong units who gain more value from adding another equipped fighter.
World Fighters players who spend a lot of time training and want better scaling between zones.
World Fighters players chasing Mythic and Secret units from stars, banners, and gacha systems.
Heavy World Fighters banner farmers who want to stack luck value for rare unit hunting.
Low-spend World Fighters players who want a cheaper way to improve gacha sessions.
World Fighters players who already farm enough gems and want faster banner sessions.
World Fighters players who spend long sessions rolling stars and want less downtime.
World Fighters players who farm far from a star location and want smoother banner access.
World Fighters players farming bosses, keys, or update routes where extra drops improve repeat runs.
World Fighters players who want a general premium pass after buying direct farming or combat upgrades.
World Fighters players who need a cheap inventory comfort upgrade.
World Fighters players who collect many units, pets, or resources and want more room.

worldFightersUpdatesAndPatchNotes.title: World Fighters Updates and Patch Notes
worldFightersUpdatesAndPatchNotes.subtitle: Follow the World Fighters update cycle, including Release PT2, Release PT3, Update 1, QoL changes, raids, new units, and code waves.
worldFightersUpdatesAndPatchNotes.intro: World Fighters has moved quickly from release codes into larger update drops, with milestone rewards, new areas, Tempest Federation content, raid-style gameplay, and QoL improvements. This timeline helps players see what changed and what to do next.

items.label: Launch Release
highlights: World Fighters launched as an anime-themed Roblox simulator by StarX Inc.
Core loop focuses on defeating enemies, collecting Cristals, and unlocking avatars and pets.
Early release codes gave potions and gems to help new players start faster.
items.player_focus: Use release rewards early, clear the first zones, and build a basic World Fighters team before spending heavily on banners.

items.label: Release Patch
highlights: Release Patch reward codes added more potions, gems, and Dragon Balls Keys.
Shutdown and milestone codes helped World Fighters players recover resources after early server restarts.
Potions became a common reward pattern across active codes.
items.player_focus: Redeem patch and shutdown codes before farming so Power, Crystal, Damage, and Luck Potion boosts are ready for active sessions.

items.label: Release PT2
highlights: Release PT2 continued the early World Fighters update cycle with new reward codes.
The RELEASEPT2 code rewarded one of each potion, 100 gems, and five Dragon Balls Keys.
Player milestones around visits, likes, favorites, and CCU continued to trigger new code drops.
items.player_focus: Use Dragon Balls Keys and gems around World Fighters banner sessions, especially when Luck Potion boosts are active.

items.label: Update 1: Tempest Federation
highlights: Tempest Federation added a new World Fighters progression zone.
The update introduced 6 new enemies, 6 new units, a new star banner, and a new secret unit.
Tempest upgrades added Attack Distance, Attack Speed, Damage, Power, and Crystal efficiency.
The rename system added random power multiplier value for units.
items.player_focus: Farm the new zone first, upgrade range for raid performance, and use rename boosts on your strongest World Fighters units.

items.label: Update 1: Tempest Invasion Raids
highlights: Tempest Invasion added a World Fighters raid-style mode that requires Slime Keys.
The mode features wave progression up to 100 waves.
Enemies approach from multiple directions while the player holds the center.
Rewards scale with wave progress and support F2P account growth.
items.player_focus: Farm Slime Keys, push wave milestones, then reinvest rewards into upgrades, gacha, awakenings, and stronger World Fighters units.

items.label: Update 1: QoL and F2P Improvements
highlights: Gems were added to chests for better F2P progression.
New music and sound effects were added.
Mount dash and position-saving improvements made World Fighters farming smoother.
Bug fixes reduced crashes and kicks.
items.player_focus: Use the smoother World Fighters farming loop to stay in long sessions, farm chests, and prepare for deeper raid attempts.

items.label: Release PT3 Code Wave
highlights: The World Fighters game title highlights Release PT3.
Multiple Release PT3-era codes added potions, gems, and Dragon Balls Keys.
New milestone codes covered visits, likes, favorites, and concurrent player milestones.
items.player_focus: Redeem the newest World Fighters code wave before grinding, then spend gems and keys during focused gacha sessions.

faq.title: World Fighters FAQ
faq.subtitle: Quick answers for World Fighters codes, progression, Roblox links, and gamepass choices.

questions.question: What is World Fighters?
questions.answer: World Fighters is an anime-inspired Roblox fighting simulator from StarX Inc where players defeat enemies, collect Cristals, unlock avatars and pets, and move through new zones.

questions.question: Where do I redeem World Fighters codes?
questions.answer: Open World Fighters on Roblox, press Shop, scroll to the code box, paste a code exactly as shown, and press Redeem.

questions.question: What rewards do World Fighters codes give?
questions.answer: World Fighters codes can give Power Potion, Crystal Potion, Damage Potion, Luck Potion, Gems, Dragon Balls Keys, Saiyan Keys, and special rewards.

questions.question: What should beginners do first in World Fighters?
questions.answer: New World Fighters players should redeem codes, farm enemies for Cristals, use boosts during active grinding, unlock avatars and pets, and complete map quests to reach new zones.

questions.question: Where is the World Fighters Discord?
questions.answer: The World Fighters Discord is available through the public invite at discord.gg/worldfighters.

questions.question: Which World Fighters gamepasses are best early?
questions.answer: The strongest early World Fighters gamepass value comes from direct progression boosts such as 2x Damage, 2x Power, 2x Crystals, and Extra Drops.

cta.title: Ready to Play World Fighters?
cta.description: Join the World Fighters Discord for codes, update notes, raid tips, and community progression help, then launch the official Roblox experience.
